---
title: "08 · VCF Mutation Analysis"
subtitle: "Mutation frequency, Shannon entropy heatmaps, and tissue-type comparisons from VCF files"
description: |
  Processes per-sample VCF files produced by a variant caller. Applies coverage filtering,
  calculates Shannon entropy per genomic position, produces mutation frequency bar charts
  and entropy heatmaps per tissue type, and runs Kruskal–Wallis tests to compare
  mutation frequencies across tissue sources.
categories: [genomics, virology, VCF, mutation, entropy]
execute:
  engine: jupyter
  kernel: jonsson-bioinfo
---

## Overview

| Item | Details |
|------|---------|
| **Input** | Directory of `.vcf` files: `aa_change_{ID}-{Source}_{Segment}_{Threshold}.vcf` |
| **Key packages** | `vcfpy`, `pandas`, `scipy`, `seaborn`, `matplotlib`, `sklearn` |
| **Statistics** | Shannon entropy (natural log) · Kruskal–Wallis non-parametric comparison |
| **Output** | Mutation bar charts · entropy heatmaps (SVG) · frequency tables · KW results CSV |
| **Download** | [template.ipynb](template.ipynb) |

::: {.callout-tip}
**When to use this template:** You have per-sample VCF files from a variant-calling
pipeline and want to compare intrahost mutation diversity across tissue types or
experimental groups. The VCF files must follow the naming convention:
`aa_change_{SampleID}-{Source}_{Segment}_{Threshold}.vcf`
:::

::: {.callout-note}
**Segment configuration:** Edit `SEGMENT_CONFIG` in the USER CONFIGURATION cell to
match your pathogen's genome (total length, CDS boundaries, amino acid count per segment).
Tissue types to include in the analysis are controlled by `TISSUE_TYPES`; all others
in the VCF filenames will be loaded but excluded from statistical comparisons.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
**Environment:** Run `conda env create -f environment.yml && conda activate jonsson-bioinfo`
before rendering. The `vcfpy` package is only available via conda/pip.
:::

---

```{python}
#| label: simulate
#| include: false
import subprocess, sys
subprocess.run([sys.executable, "data/simulate_data.py"], check=True)
```

```{python}
#| child: template.ipynb
```
